description: When something is impossible, assuming it leads straight to disaster.

guides.text: The goal is $\lnot A \to (A \to \bot)$. In words: if $\lnot A$ is true, then supposing $A$ lands you in a [contradiction|contradiction] ($\bot$, pronounced 'bottom' or 'falsity' — we'll meet its dual, $\top$ 'top', in a later problem). This is what [negation|negation] **means** in our system — $\lnot A$ is the claim that $A$ [implication|implies] falsity.

As always with an [implication|implication], assume the left-hand side. This time it's $\lnot A$ — a compound [formula|formula] we haven't worked with before. The engine parses `(not A)` as $\lnot A$.

The rule `not-elim` converts a [negation|negation] into the [implication|implication] it stands for. From $\lnot A$ at step 1, it produces $A \to \bot$ — '$A$ implies falsity'. The [context|context] stays the same.
We now have $A \to \bot$ under the [assumption|assumption] $\lnot A$. Use `->-intro` to discharge and finish.

unlocks.name: not-elim
unlocks.description: From $\lnot p$ derive $p \to \bot$ — negation unwraps into an implication to falsity.
